title: alnus viridis var. parvifolia
intro: Alnus viridis var. parvifolia, also known as green alder, is a variety of the green alder plant that is native to North America. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The leaves and bark of the green alder contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of green alder, such as teas and infusions, can be used to promote health and well-being, and may also be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
